items.title: CVE-2026-23111: Linux Kernel nf_tables Use-After-Free — Working Exploit Published June 8
items.summary: A single inverted '!' operator in Linux kernel nf_tables allows an unprivileged local user to escalate to root and escape containers. Patched Feb 5 2026, but Exodus Intelligence published a full working exploit on June 8, with an independent PoC already out from FuzzingLabs since April. Affects Debian Bookworm/Trixie and Ubuntu 22.04/24.04 LTS.
items.why: Container escape + local privilege escalation with a public working exploit is critical for any containerized workload on unpatched kernels. Runtime security controls and kernel versions need immediate review — any unpatched Debian/Ubuntu host is one rogue process away from full root.

items.title: CVE-2026-20182: Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN CVSS 10.0 Auth Bypass — Sixth SD-WAN Zero-Day of 2026
items.summary: A perfect-CVSS-10.0 authentication bypass in Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Controller and Manager lets an unauthenticated remote attacker gain admin privileges by abusing the vdaemon service over DTLS (UDP 12346), then injecting SSH keys into vmanage-admin. CISA added it to KEV; Cisco confirmed active limited exploitation in May 2026.
items.why: Full network fabric takeover is the blast radius: an attacker with admin on SD-WAN Manager controls routing policy for every branch. This is the sixth SD-WAN zero-day exploited in 2026 — a pattern suggesting targeted, sustained campaign against SD-WAN management planes.
items.source: Cisco PSIRT / The Hacker News / CISA KEV

items.title: CVE-2025-48595: Android Framework Zero-Click Bug (CVSS 8.4) Actively Exploited — June 2026 Patch Released
items.summary: Google's June 2026 Android Security Bulletin patches 124 flaws; the most urgent is CVE-2025-48595, a high-severity Android Framework vulnerability (CVSS 8.4) requiring zero user interaction, confirmed under active exploitation by sophisticated threat actors. All devices below June 2026 patch level are exposed.
items.why: Zero-interaction exploitation means compromise is possible without a single tap — no phishing click needed. Enterprise MDM policies should force-push the June patch immediately, especially for devices handling sensitive comms or credentials.
items.source: Google Android Security / Help Net Security

items.title: Unit 42: Iranian APT 'Screening Serpens' Deploys 6 New RAT Variants Using .NET AppDomainManager Hijacking
items.summary: Palo Alto Networks Unit 42 tracked Iranian state-sponsored APT 'Screening Serpens' deploying two new malware families — MiniUpdate and MiniJunk V2 — across US, Israeli, and UAE aerospace, defense, and telecom targets since the Feb 28 2026 regional conflict. The group weaponizes AppDomainManager hijacking to force .NET applications to disable their own security controls at initialization.
items.why: AppDomainManager hijacking is a living-off-the-land .NET technique that bypasses EDR detections tuned for Office macro attacks. High-tempo ops (6 new RAT variants in 10 weeks) signals strong resourcing. Aerospace, defense manufacturing, and telecom are the stated target sectors.

items.title: Verizon DBIR 2026: Vulnerability Exploitation Dethrones Credential Theft as #1 Breach Vector for First Time in 19 Years
items.summary: The 2026 Verizon DBIR (31,000 incidents, 22,000+ confirmed breaches — nearly double last year) finds unpatched vulnerability exploitation now drives 31% of breaches, overtaking credential abuse (13%) as the leading initial access vector for the first time in the report's 19-year history. Confirmed breaches almost doubled year-over-year.
items.why: This structural shift means organizations over-indexed on MFA and identity controls are now more exposed than those with disciplined patch management. The 19-year historical baseline makes this statistically robust — security investment priorities should shift accordingly.
